_note: The Puranic Solar & Lunar dynasties of India — a SEPARATE Indian tradition, deliberately NOT connected to Adam's line. It is its own island root, like the Sumerian King List file. Sources: the Ramayana, Mahabharata, Vishnu Purana, Harivamsha and Bhagavata Purana genealogies (see Wikipedia 'Solar dynasty', 'Lunar dynasty', 'List of Ikshvaku dynasty kings'). Vaivasvata Manu is the flood-survivor of Hindu tradition — warned by the fish-avatar Matsya, he rode out the deluge in a boat, making him India's Noah and a direct parallel to Ziusudra and Noah in the other flood traditions. From Manu the line BRANCHES into two royal houses: the SOLAR dynasty (Suryavamsha) through his son Ikshvaku, culminating in Rama; and the LUNAR dynasty (Chandravamsha) through his daughter Ila (by her consort Budha), culminating in Puru, Bharata and Kuru. EVERYTHING here is legendary/mythological — the traditional chronology is elastic and non-historical, and the dates (c.2900–2000 BC) are approximate placeholders chosen only so the chain renders top-to-bottom; they are NOT historical claims. Fixed-generation gaps are compressed (only the famous kings are shown). All ids prefixed IN_ so they classify to the INDIA house.

a.label:
Vaivasvata Manu
c.2900 BC · the flood-survivor warned by the fish-avatar Matsya — India's Noah; progenitor of both royal houses · legendary/mythological

children.label:
Ikshvaku
c.2850 BC · Manu's son; founder of the Solar dynasty (Suryavamsha), granted Aryavarta · legendary

Ila
c.2850 BC · Manu's daughter; matriarch of the Lunar dynasty (Chandravamsha) · legendary

Vikukshi
c.2800 BC · son of Ikshvaku; also called Shashada · legendary

Mandhata
c.2700 BC · great conqueror & chakravartin of the Solar line · legendary

Harishchandra
c.2600 BC · the truthful king who never told a lie · legendary

Sagara
c.2500 BC · king whose 60,000 sons were burned to ash by the sage Kapila · legendary

Bhagiratha
c.2400 BC · brought the river Ganga (Ganges) down from heaven to earth · legendary

Raghu
c.2250 BC · so illustrious the line is called the Raghuvamsha (house of Raghu) · legendary

Aja
c.2200 BC · son of Raghu; grandfather of Rama · legendary

Dasharatha
c.2150 BC · king of Ayodhya; father of Rama · legendary

b.label:
Kausalya
chief queen of Dasharatha; mother of Rama · legendary

children.label:
Rama
c.2100 BC · seventh avatar of Vishnu; hero of the Ramayana — the great descendant of the Solar dynasty · legendary

b.label:
Budha
consort of Ila; son of Chandra (the Moon), from whom the Lunar dynasty takes its name · legendary

children.label:
Pururavas
c.2800 BC · first king of the Lunar dynasty (Chandravamsha) · legendary

b.label:
Urvashi
the apsara; consort of Pururavas · legendary

children.label:
Ayus
c.2750 BC · son of Pururavas · legendary

Nahusha
c.2700 BC · king who briefly usurped the throne of Indra · legendary

Yayati
c.2650 BC · emperor whose five sons founded the great clans · legendary

b.label:
Sharmishtha
consort of Yayati; mother of Puru · legendary

children.label:
Puru
c.2600 BC · youngest son of Yayati; founder of the Paurava (Puru) line · legendary

Bharata
c.2500 BC · emperor after whom India is named 'Bharata'; of the Puru line · legendary (generations compressed)

Kuru
c.2400 BC · founder of the Kuru clan; ancestor of the Pandavas & Kauravas of the Mahabharata · legendary
